paper.title: Ancient, eclipsing, tidally-locked: A blue lurker progenitor in the population of extreme-velocity star candidates

extraction.summary: The paper selected J1903-0023 (Gaia DR3 4265540383431508736) as a late-type HVS candidate from Gaia DR3 and performed follow-up spectroscopy and kinematic analysis. Both J1903-0023 and the comparison star WISE J072543.88-235119.7 were explicitly concluded to be bound to the Galaxy. No objects are presented as possibly unbound from the Milky Way.

method_chain.summary: Gaia DR3 catalog query for fast stars.

source_refs.context: Gaia DR3 catalog query and target selection

method_chain.summary: Selection of late-type HVS candidates with 1-sigma lower tangential velocity limit of 800 km/s and colour criterion GBP-GRP > 0.7 mag.

source_refs.context: Sample selection criteria and candidate identification

method_chain.summary: Atmospheric parameter determination from X-shooter spectroscopy using ATLAS12/SYNTHE model grids and global chi-squared minimization.
outputs: Teff, log g, [Fe/H], [alpha/Fe], v sin i, radial velocity

method_chain.summary: SED fitting with synthetic models to derive angular diameter, reddening, and stellar temperature from combined photometric magnitudes.
outputs: Angular diameter, colour excess E(44-55), effective temperature

method_chain.summary: Radial velocity measurements from X-shooter, NOT/ALFOSC, and SOAR/Goodman spectrographs.

source_refs.context: Radial velocity follow-up spectroscopy with X-shooter, NOT/ALFOSC, and SOAR/Goodman

method_chain.summary: Spectro-photometric distances derived from angular diameter and spectroscopic radius.

source_refs.context: Distance derivation from angular diameter and radius

method_chain.summary: Calculation of Galactocentric velocities from spectroscopic distances, radial velocities, and proper motions.

source_refs.context: Trajectory calculation from spectroscopic distances, radial velocities, and proper motions

method_chain.summary: Adopted Model 1, a revised version of the Allen (1991) Galactic potential.
Numerical orbit integration using a 4th-order Runge-Kutta solver back in time for 15 Gyr.

source_refs.context: Numerical integration of equations of motion using a 4th-order Runge-Kutta solver

method_chain.summary: Bound/unbound assessment from orbital integration and velocity comparison against Galactic escape velocity.

candidate_groups_considered.description: J1903-0023 (Gaia DR3 4265540383431508736), the primary target selected as a late-type HVS candidate from Gaia DR3 with high tangential velocity.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper explicitly concludes that J1903-0023 is bound to the Galaxy. The spectroscopic distance is significantly smaller than the Gaia parallax-based distance, resulting in a lower tangential velocity (585 +/- 40 km/s) and a bound orbit.

source_refs.context: Abstract stating the star is bound to the Galaxy
Kinematic section concluding the star is bound

candidate_groups_considered.description: WISE J072543.88-235119.7 (J0725-2351), an extreme-velocity sdF star analysed as a twin comparison star.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents WISE J0725-2351 as a bound halo comparison star, not as a candidate possibly unbound from the Galaxy. Its Galactic rest-frame velocity is 240 km/s and its orbit is typical for halo stars.

source_refs.context: Kinematic description of WISE J0725-2351 as a halo star with 240 km/s rest-frame velocity
Orbit description as typical for halo stars
